summary: First iteration. Backend: 18/18 pytest tests passed against public ingress URL (products + filters, baskets, courses, register/login/me/logout, empresa registration, course enrollment + auth gate, discount logic for anon/volume/loyalty/empresa, checkout creates order and increments loyalty, urban gardens register + listing privacy). Frontend: home + navbar load, navigation to /catalogo /cestas /formacion /huertos works, add-to-cart updates badge, /carrito shows items, login redirects to /dashboard which shows orders/enrollments/loyalty (250 -> 274 after order), checkout shows correct discount (4% silver loyalty) + delivery fee + total, place order navigates to /pedido/:id success page, navbar logout clears cookies and protected /dashboard route bounces to /login.

issues: HTML <title> is the default 'Emergent | Fullstack App' instead of an ECO-CULTURE branded title in /app/frontend/public/index.html

action_items: (Optional/minor) Update <title> in /app/frontend/public/index.html to 'ECO-CULTURE — Verduras y hortalizas ecológicas' for branding/SEO.
(Code review) /api/checkout currently allows anonymous orders (user_id=null) — ensure that is intentional; if checkout should require auth, add Depends(get_current_user).
critical_code_review_comments: CORS uses allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=False which is fine for same-origin ingress, but if domain ever differs cookies won't be sent — document this constraint.
Cookies set with secure=False; ingress is HTTPS so secure=True + samesite='none' would be safer, but works in current preview environment.
/api/checkout silently allows anonymous orders (user_id=null) — confirm product intent; likely should require auth.
compute_discount uses int(total) for loyalty points so partial euros are dropped — acceptable but document.
No rate-limiting / brute-force lockout on /auth/login (playbook mentions lockout after 5 fails) — consider adding.
test_credentials: cliente@ecoculture.es / Cliente2026! and admin@ecoculture.es / EcoAdmin2026! (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md). Both work via POST /api/auth/login.
seed_data_creation: Seed already runs at backend startup (admin, test client with 250 loyalty points, 12 products, 4 baskets, 6 courses). Tests created additional ephemeral users with TEST_/random emails and one TEST_ urban garden — non-destructive.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 18 backend pytest cases in /app/backend/tests/test_ecoculture_api.py pass. Frontend full purchase + auth + logout flow validated end-to-end. No blocking issues. Note: console shows expected 401s from AuthContext probing /api/auth/me when logged out — not a bug. Test user's loyalty_points will keep growing each run as checkout test creates real orders; reset DB if absolute counts matter.
